import logging

class CustomFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    COLOR_CODES = {
        logging.DEBUG: "\x1b[34;20m",   # Blue
        logging.INFO: "\x1b[32;20m",    # Green
        logging.WARNING: "\x1b[33;20m", # Yellow
        logging.ERROR: "\x1b[31;20m",   # Red
        logging.CRITICAL: "\x1b[31;1m"  # Bold Red
    }
    RESET_CODE = "\x1b[0m"

    BASE_FORMAT = "[%(asctime)s] [%(levelname)-8s] %(name)s (%(filename)s:%(lineno)d) - %(message)s"
    DATE_FORMAT = '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'

    def __init__(self, fmt=BASE_FORMAT, datefmt=DATE_FORMAT, style='%'):
        super().__init__(fmt=fmt, datefmt=datefmt, style=style)

    def format(self, record):
        formatted_message = super().format(record)

        level_color = self.COLOR_CODES.get(record.levelno)
        if level_color:
            return f"{level_color}{formatted_message}{self.RESET_CODE}"
        else:
            return formatted_message

if __name__ == "__main__":
    logger = logging.getLogger("my_application")
    logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

    ch = logging.StreamHandler()
    ch.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

    custom_formatter = CustomFormatter()
    ch.setFormatter(custom_formatter)

    logger.addHandler(ch)

    logger.debug("This is a debug message.")
    logger.info("This is an info message.")
    logger.warning("This is a warning message.")
    logger.error("This is an error message.")
    logger.critical("This is a critical message.")

    try:
        result = 1 / 0
    except ZeroDivisionError:
        logger.exception("An error occurred during division.")
